Join Megan Snider, author and Founder of Appleseed Yoga for Kids as she delivers inspiring stories and gets the whole family moving. Class begins and ends with song and breathwork and is filled with pages of creativity and activity. Kids and families can sit quietly and listen, do partial poses or make up their own, following our lead. It's ALL yoga! No experience, mats, or material

events.title: L'Heure du conte / French Storytime
events.description: Rejoignez-nous pour des histoires, des chansons et des comptines en français pour les enfants âgés de 0 à 5 ans, accompagnés de leurs parents et responsables. Les familles sont invitées à rester et à jouer pour 30 minutes supplémentaires après la conclusion du programme. Enjoy stories, songs and rhymes in French at a staff-led story time for children age 0-5 with their parents and caregivers. Families are welcome to "stay and play" for an additional 30 minutes following the program. -------

Ages 3-9. Drop-in.

events.title: Unconventional Materials: An Osborne Collection Exhibit
events.description: An exhibition of intricately crafted books, artworks and much more. The Unconventional Materials exhibit highlights the use of unconventional techniques and materials in children’s literature, featuring historical and modern examples.   Visit the Osborne Collection to experience our exhibit featuring items such as plasticine art by Barbara Reid, dioramas and panoramas, postcards and other ephemera, and hornbooks and battledores. Located on the 4th floor of the Lillian H. Smith Branch. Free.

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: LEAP After School Program
events.description: This  program offers a fun and engaging environment with two hours of physical activities, trivia, games, crafts, and sports-based activities. Participants will build independence and social skills while enjoying nutritious snacks and educational workshops. The program is safe, supervised, and facilitated in partnership with North York Community House LEAP staff. events.title: Scrapbooking
events.description: This program discuses Library of Congress women’s suffrage primary sources, such as letters, diaries, women’s self-published information sources and other ephemera, to examine how suffragists documented their activism and feelings on women’s rights and social issues of the time. Program attendees use current magazines, newspapers, flyers, ephemera and personal effects to start their own scrapbook and create their own DIY personal history (that might become a future primary source!).

events.description: LEGO Free Play is a fun, creative program where children can build anything they imagine using LEGO bricks. Drop-in program, no registration required.

events.description: Want to learn how to knit or crochet with a fun group of people? Looking for help with your project? You can also share your projects with others, teach or learn a technique, and enjoy great conversation. All levels of expertise are welcome. Bring your own yarn, needles, and crochet hooks. All are welcome. Drop-in, no registration necessary.

events.title: STEAM Saturday: Rigamajig
events.description: Join us for a hands-on building adventure with Rigamajig — a giant creative construction set made of wooden planks, wheels, pulleys, and bolts! events.title: From the Golden Age to the Modern Era: A Musical Odyssey of East Asia
events.description: This concert introduces the history of the yangqin and its diverse regional and folk traditions, guiding the audience on a musical journey across China through sound, storytelling, and performance. This performance will include selections from different eras and styles, along with brief introductions to provide an overview of the musical journey. events.description: This interactive puppet show from Chad Solomon features characters from the  Rabbit and Bear Paws children's graphic novel series. In this performance, audiences will experience the story of how the turtle carried our world on her back, and how the tiny muskrat succeeded in giving us the greatest gift of all - a place to call home, Turtle Island. Registering will register your child for all 4 sessions.

Suitable for 6 years and up.  Please note: this program is for children with adult accompaniment. This program is suitable for children 6 years old and above. events.title: Make, Create and Discover!
events.description: Join us for fun, hands-on activities. For children ages 6-12 years. Drop-in. Space is limited. First come first served. July 7 - Button Art July 28 - Nature Explorers August 4 - Science Explorers Lab August 11 - Rock Painting August 18 - Dioramas and Collages

events.title: Explorers Club
events.description: Let's travel the globe through various stories, crafts, and hands-on activities at the library! Ages 6-12. Drop-in.

events.title: Lego Club
events.description: Every Wednesday come out to build, create and use your imagination with LEGO. Enjoy a new building challenge each week. Complete 5 challenges and get a sticker! events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: Board Games for Kids
events.description: Jenga! Monopoly! Ticket to Ride! And More! Spend the afternoon playing different board games with your friends!  Snacks are offered during this program for participants. Ingredient lists will be available. Snacks are generously supported, in part, by donors.

events.title: STEM Programming For Children
events.description: Come and enjoy after school robotics and snap circuit programs!

events.title: Chess Club
events.description: Drop in for a casual game of chess in a welcoming environment. Improve your chess skills or learn how to play. Sets are provided. All ages. Drop-in.

events.title: Boba Drink Making Workshop
events.description: Chef Yumiko leads a hands-on workshop to make the popular Asian beverage, Brown Sugar Oat Milk with Boba. Participants will learn to prepare and use the classics ingredients including oat milk and tapioca pearls to make this visually pleasing and sweet drink. Chef Yumiko will also provide a brief history of Boba and why it's so popular globally.

events.title: Storytelling and Afrikan Drumming
events.description: Storyteller and professinal percussionist, Kwanza Msingwana, performs folktales and traditional stories from Afrika and around the world. This is a drop-in program. No registration is required.

events.title: NatureTO: Trees Around Taylor Memorial
events.description: Taylor Memorial Branch and its neighbourhood is home to a variety of trees. Join the City of Toronto Urban Forestry staff to explore the natural world on a local neighbourhood tour. Learn how to recognize different species and fun facts about their cultural and ecological importance. Children under 12 must be accompanied by a parent/caregiver. Registration is required   The tour will start and end at the Taylor Memorial meeting room and will be guided by the City of Toronto U

events.title: Children's Book Club
events.description: Meet other kids, talk about books and engage in fun activities, all in a friendly environment. Make sure to pick up a copy of the book in advance at the children's information desk. Suitable for children ages 8-12. events.title: Crafturday
events.description: Join us for a day of fun and crafts. Partake in a new and engaging craft every Saturday from 9:30am - 4:30pm. Drop-in and self-serve. For pre-school and school-aged children and their caregivers. If your child lives with a disability, and requires an accessibility accommodation to participate in this program, please contact Accessibility Services at accessibleservices@tpl.ca or phone 416-393-7099. Please contact us at least three weeks in advance.

events.title: Builder's Club
events.description: Join us on Saturday mornings and build to your heart’s content!  LEGO, DUPLO, and KEVA planks will be provided for you to explore your creativity. All children welcome with their caregivers. Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: Blocks Challenges
events.description: Join us on a Saturday morning for a building activity! Complete the challenges using Mega Bloks and Lego Duplo blocks. For pre-school and school-aged children and their caregivers. Drop-in and self-serve. Unfortunately, we cannot accommodate any large groups/daycares for this program. If you or your child lives with a disability, and requires an accessibility accommodation to participate in this program, please contact Accessibility Services by email accessibleservices@tpl.ca , or voicemail, 416

events.title: Saturday Chess Club
events.description: Join us on the 2nd floor every Saturday for a few games of chess in a welcoming environment. All ages and all levels are welcome. Children must be accompanied by a parent or guardian to participate. Any unsupervised individuals under 18 will not be able to participate in the program. This is a drop-in program. Please note that there will be no chess club on Saturday May 16 for our Big Book Sale.

events.title: Lego Free Play
events.description: LEGO Free Play is a fun, creative program where children can build anything they imagine using LEGO bricks. There are no instructions or rules to follow, kids are free to explore, create, and play at their own pace. This open-ended play encourages creativity, problem-solving, and social interaction. Drop-in program, no registration required.

events.title: Scientists in School: Science Meets Art
events.description: Discover what happens when science meets art! Explore perception as you use shadows, colour, motion, and light to create art from science. You’ll make a puppet, a spinning top and a kaleidoscope, as well as design your own galaxy. “The greatest scientists are always artists as well!” (Albert Einstein) Please note: this program is suitable for children 4 years old and above with adult accompaniment. From building simple robots and experimenting with Snap Circuits to creative design challenges and more, there’s always something new to discover. Activities change each week, so there’s always something new to explore!

events.title: Summer Wonder Camp: Presented by Dino Bites
events.description: Come join us at the library for a 90 min workshop for 3 days! Handle and discuss real and replica fossils of dinosaurs and other extinct beasts from all over the world. Discover the wild world of dinosaurs with an expert and learn about fossils evolution and the ‘Tree of Life.' Presented by Dino Bites. Ages 4-12.

events.description: Join us for a fun and engaging workshop with award-winning author Melanie Florence. Learn techniques for brainstorming and transforming your creative ideas into stories. We'll explore plotting and characters so you can improve your writing skills and craft amazing stories of your own.  Ages 9-12. No registration required.

events.title: Photo Editing with Adobe Photoshop
events.description: Adobe Photoshop is the world’s best imaging and graphic design software and is at the core of just about every creative project, from photo editing and compositing to digital painting, animation, and graphic design. Join us for an introductory lesson and learn about the most common tools. We'll explore the workspace and enhance images while investigating tools such as crop, levels, colour balance and more. See how an image can be altered and repaired using patch tools such as the clone and heali

events.title: Youth Hub: Needle Felting Basics
events.description: Want to learn how to needle felt? Weston Youth Hub will be hosting a needle felting basics program where participants' will learn how to transform wool into their own creations! This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: History of Generative Music: The Early Modern Era
events.description: The twentieth century brought radical new ideas about what music could be - from compositions determined by coin tosses to pieces that rearranged themselves with every performance. Pioneering composers began using chance, repetition, and early electronics to challenge everything audiences thought they knew about music. In this session, you'll explore how early modern composers pushed the boundaries of generative music, discover the creative and technical mechanisms behind their experiments, and

events.title: Japanese Tea Ceremony
events.description: Experience the traditional art of the Japanese tea ceremony. Learn about Japanese culture and the important philosophy behind the tea ceremony. This presentation not only offers a demonstration of traditional Matcha preparation using authentic tools, but zen philosophies which may also apply to daily life. Originating in the Southern part of India, the performance will highlight beautiful music, costume and story telling using hand gestures and expressions.  Drop-in All Ages. The program will take place in the Auditorium located in the basement. Elevator on site. If you have any questions, please call Bendale Branch at 416-396-8910.

events.title: Scrapbooking
events.description: This program discuses Library of Congress women’s suffrage primary sources, such as letters, diaries, women’s self-published information sources and other ephemera, to examine how suffragists documented their activism and feelings on women’s rights and social issues of the time. Program attendees use current magazines, newspapers, flyers, ephemera and personal effects to start their own scrapbook and create their own DIY personal history (that might become a future primary source!).

This lively and fascinating account of a military expedition of over 600 Toronto men to Edwardian England, was paid for by Sir Henry Pellatt, the wealthy Canadian financier who later realized his dream of building the Casa Loma castle. events.title: Indigenous Film Screening - The Stand
events.description: On a misty morning in the fall of 1985, a small group of Haida people blockaded a muddy dirt road on Lyell Island, demanding the government work with Indigenous people to find a way to protect the land and the future. In a riveting new feature documentary drawn from more than a hundred hours of archival footage and audio, award-winning director Christopher Auchter ( Now Is the Time ) recreates the critical moment when the Haida Nation’s resolute act of vision and conscience changed the world. events.description: Join us on the 2nd floor every Saturday for a few games of chess in a welcoming environment. All ages and all levels are welcome. Children must be accompanied by a parent or guardian to participate. Any unsupervised individuals under 18 will not be able to participate in the program. This is a drop-in program. Please note that there will be no chess club on Saturday May 16 for our Big Book Sale.

events.title: Instructional Chess
events.description: The Chess Institute of Canada is bringing chess to life at your local library! Join us as we explore the ancient game of chess and the essential life-skills the game imparts. Come and take in a lesson on the great game or sit down and start playing!  Learn, compete, connect with your community through the game of chess. Open to all ages. Children under 12 need to be supervised.

events.title: Create Pop Music with AI Tools for Teens
events.description: Have you ever had a melody stuck in your head and wished you could turn it into a real song? AI tools are making it easier than ever to bring your musical experiences to life - but how do you make sure the music you create is truly yours? Explore how to use AI as a creative assistant to build radio-ready pop tracks based on your own original ideas. You'll learn how to guide AI tools to serve your vision rather than replace it, and we'll cover Canadian copyright guidelines to ensure the music you

events.title: How to Become a Volunteer
events.description: Interested in learning more about volunteering opportunities in Toronto? This session is intended for the volunteer seekers who are new to volunteering, or in need to understand the process of formal volunteering. Join us to learn how and where to search for opportunities that best match your interests and what to expect after you apply for a volunteer role. This session caters to all demographics. Presented by Volunteer Toronto .

events.title: Music Copyright and Ethics in the AI Era
events.description: When you use AI to create music, who actually owns it? Can a melody generated by a machine infringe on someone else's copyright? As AI tools become more accessible, these questions are more relevant than ever - and the answers aren't always straightforward. In this session, you'll get a plain-language breakdown of how music copyright works, what the rules are around sampling, and how these long-standing principles apply to AI-generated music. You'll also hear about landmark legal cases - both su

events.title: The Future of AI in Entertainment with James Rowan
events.description: Generative AI is rapidly reshaping how creative work is imagined, produced, and shared. From image and video generation to writing, design, and virtual production, these tools are expanding what's possible while also raising important questions about authorship, labour, and creative identity. April 16: Meet the Parents (2000, PG) April 23: Rounders (1998, R) April 30: No Country for Old Men (2007, 18A) May 7: Apollo 13 (1995, PG) May 14: The Martian (2015, PG) * special start time! 5:30 pm May 21: Contact (1997, PG) * special start time! 5:30 pm May 28: Star Trek (2009, PG) All are welcome. Drop-in, no registration required.

events.description: Have you ever wondered how we are able to measure distances in the Solar System, in our Galaxy, and beyond? events.title: NatureTO: Trees Around  the Branch Tour
events.description: The North York Central Library and its neighbourhood is home to a variety of trees. Join the City of Toronto Urban Forestry staff to explore the natural world on a local neighbourhood walk. Learn how to recognize different species and fun facts about their cultural and ecological importance. Children under 12 must be accompanied by a parent/caregiver. Please note, the tour will start and end at the North York Central Library, Room 1, and will be guided by the City of Toronto Urban Forestry staff

events.title: Pawsitively Pets: Hands-on Animal Education
events.description: Pawsitively Pets is a hands-on animal education program for children of all ages. Our wonderful animal team is made up of an assortment of rescued pets that have found their forever home with us. Our goal is to provide our animal team with a permanent home and the best possible care, while sharing our love or animals with others. events.title: Botanical Photography / Sunprints
events.description: An alternative eco-friendly photography process that uses botanicals as both its subject matter and development process. I will teach participants how to make their own simple chemical-free photography developer and discuss alternative negatives. Participants will be encouraged to use bring plants from home, leaves found on site, etc to use for the process. This safe chemistry-free activity is adaptable for a broad group of ages Led by Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Youth Hub:  Nail Night
events.description: Unleash your creativity as you design your own stunning nail art! Join us for one or all of our fabulous Nail Nights on the first Monday of every month. Drop-in, no registration required. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: The Hidden Rivers
events.description: Participants will learn about the hidden rivers in Toronto and explore how our changing cityscape impacts on our waterways and ecosystems. Looking at archival images and current references, participants will be guided to bring their own stories into our city’s images using material such as watercolours, crayons, playdough, text, etc onto colouring pages. Led by Shannyn Porter and Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Retirement Planning
events.description: Presented by Riverdale Immigrant Women’s Centre (RIWC) , this workshop is an introduction to long-term financial planning including retirement savings and better understanding of federal income benefits, such as, Canada Pension Plan (CPP) and Old Age Security (OAS). Registration encouraged . Drop-ins are welcome until capacity is reached.

events.title: Book Club
events.description: Come and join us on the third Thursday of every other month to discuss a variety of books. Everyone is welcome! Drop in, no registration required. This year we are reading:  February 19 -  The Strangers by Katherena Vermette April 16 -  Big Little Lies by Liane Moriarty June 18 -  On the Ravine by Vincent Lam September 17 -  Tomorrow, and Tomorrow, and Tomorrow by Gabrielle Zevin November 19 -  Small Things Like These by

Ask us for our list of video games, and check our monthly calendar for Gaming Tournaments with prizes! This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Mindful Mondays
events.description: Every Monday, enjoy a series of stress-relieving mindfulness activities, crafts, and games to help ease anxiety and give you a moment of Zen. Enjoy mindful colouring sheets, rainbow scratch art, mindfulness card decks, gratitude journals, and more. All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by

events.title: Youth Hub: Board Game Café
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Tuesday to play board games with your friends! Ask us for our list of board games, and check our monthly calendar for Tournaments with prizes! All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Study Hall Wednesdays
events.description: On Wednesdays, the Eatonville Youth Hub becomes a quiet space for teens to study, work on projects, and request homework help and resume help from staff. Bring your homework and enjoy a snack in a space that is just for teens. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: Youth Hub: Crafty Thursdays
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Thursday for a series of crafting activities like painting, clay sculpting, sewing and embroidery, resin art, jewelry making, perler beads, block printing, and more! Check tpl.ca or our monthly calendar for the activity of the week. All materials provided. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily.

events.title: Seniors E-Connect
events.description: Are you 55+? Do you have a phone, tablet or computer you don't know how to use or want to learn to use better? Do you enjoy meeting new people? Seniors E-Connect will help you learn tech topics in a fun group setting, but with the one-on-one help of a teen volunteer.

events.title: Youth Hub:  Nail Night
events.description: Unleash your creativity as you design your own stunning nail art! Join us for one or all of our fabulous Nail Nights on the first Monday of every month. Drop-in, no registration required. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.
